1.description: Catalog item fulfillment orchestrator (DOC-09 §UC-8). Generic engine that fulfils ANY catalog item by reading its template from itsm.catalog_item, decomposing the request into a task DAG via a single LLM call, and orchestrating execution through LangGraph (parallel waves via Send, approval gates via interrupt(), retry with backoff, saga compensation on cancel). Both portal-submit and chat paths land in the same handler — parity guaranteed by `tools.fulfill_request`. Mock integration adapters (AD / email / VPN / GitHub / license / procurement) swap to real APIs via the IntegrationAdapter Protocol without handler changes. Tracks SR → RITM → tasks → approvals in itsm.* substrate; emits per-task OTel spans + per-tenant cost; lifecycle-gated by step-1 state machine (ships as draft, activated by quality gate).

skills.name: Fulfill a catalog request
skills.description: Decompose and carry out fulfillment of a catalog item, service request, or onboarding - provisioning accounts, mailboxes, licenses, group/VPN access, and ordering hardware, including approval gates and rollback. Use when the user wants to OBTAIN or PROVISION something, or have an existing catalog request executed. Action-tier: mutations require confirmation/approval. NOT for summarizing a record, fetching KB/how-to articles, finding similar tickets, or triaging an existing incident.
use_when: the user asks to get, request, order, or provision an item, software, license, hardware, or access
the user wants a new hire onboarded or an environment/account set up
the user wants an existing catalog request or service request carried out or its tasks created
the user wants to set up a mailbox, group membership, VPN, or directory account for a request
the user wants the status of an in-flight fulfillment request
the user wants to obtain or provision a specific item or service from the catalog
not_when: the user wants the facts, fields, or timeline of an existing record (route to uc01_summarization)
the user wants knowledge, documentation, or how-to on doing something themselves (route to uc03_kb_lookup)
the user wants other tickets similar to or duplicates of a record (route to uc02_similar_tickets)
the user wants an existing incident classified, prioritized, or assigned (route to uc05_triage)
the user is reporting a brand-new problem/outage (that is incident creation, not catalog fulfillment)
the user seeks guidance on how to resolve an existing incident (route to uc03_kb_lookup)
examples: onboard new employee John Smith starting March 15
I need access to the finance share
set up a new AWS account for me
order a second monitor for my desk
please provision a new email account for me
request access to the marketing folder
can you fulfill my request for a new phone?

input_fields.name: query
input_fields.description: What the user wants to request (free text).
